competition.name: Stella Zhang New Venture Competition
competition.host_school: UCI Paul Merage School of Business

naming_gift.initial_gift_year_note: Initial gift announced January 2022 per Merage press release; the underlying term begins as early as the 2021 NVC.
naming_gift.naming_through_year_note: Computed as 2026 + 5 (extension term). The Merage March 2025 release describes 'a $750,000 commitment to extend her annual gift over the next five years' without naming an explicit end year.
naming_gift.bridge_to_competition: Zarina Bahadur (Baby Box, MIE '20, 2020 Grand Prize) joined Zhang's Long Beach Accelerator portfolio, exposing Zhang to the NVC.

competition.sponsors_note: Sponsors fund the program collectively. Per-track corporate sponsorship does not exist in this competition's structure; tracks are funded from the pool and named only by category.

mentor_structure.note: Coach and judge roles overlap; named individuals like Dan Jenkins, David Ochi, and Larry Wilk function as both.

sample_workshops: How to Come up with a Billion Dollar Idea
Is there a Market for Your Product?

grand_finale.venue: SB1 Merage Auditorium, 4291 Pereira Drive, Suite 2300, Irvine, CA 92627

years.prize_pool_announced: Over $100,000 plus AWS credits for all semi-finalists
years.prize_pool_note: Advertised pool was "over $100,000". The core competition cash is $100,000 (five tracks at $10,000 first and $5,000 second, plus a $20,000 Grand Prize and a $5,000 Runner-Up). Three specialty awards sit on top: the Beall Applied Innovation Specialty Award ($10,000, LAMON), the MIE Specialty Award ($2,500, Ascend First) and the Fan Favorite Award ($1,000, RestWing). The Beall Applied Innovation award is framed by Merage as a Wayfinder incubator and mentorship award; whether it is paid in cash or in-kind is not disclosed, so it is excluded from the disclosed cash total, matching this archive's exclusion of residency and in-kind awards. Confirmed cash awarded is $103,500; if the Beall award is cash the figure is $113,500.
years.finalists_note: 10 finalists per Merage NVC page as of 2026-05-12: two per track. An earlier archive pass recorded 'AegleBio, Billify, NeuroBoost, Grad2Go' as named semi-finalists; those teams do not appear on the current finalist page and could not be re-verified against a primary source on 2026-05-12.
years.judges_note: Judging panel not published in an official Merage source. The OC Startup Council recap (2026-06-12) reports the finale panel as including Isabelle Bart, Scott Joslyn, Matt Sand, Charla Triplett and a judge listed only as 'Jasmine J.'; the wording ('including') suggests the panel may have been larger. Names are single-source and not cross-verified against a primary UCI source.
years.results_note: No standalone 2026 results press release or NVC report PDF has been published yet, unlike prior years. The award table below was corroborated across the official Merage NVC page winners block, the OC Startup Council recap (2026-06-12), UCI New University coverage, UCI MIND, and the antrepreneur.uci.edu interview with Pipeline's Tyler Maher. An AI-generated search summary claiming NeuroStrat won the Grand Prize was checked and refuted: every human-written results source names Pipeline as Grand Prize winner and NeuroStrat as Runner-Up.

awards.project: AI transfer-planning platform that builds personalized term-by-term roadmaps for community college students moving to UC and CSU schools. Operates as Pipeline Planning.

pre_2017_editions.disclosed_cash_total_note: EmbryLux $20K (dual award) + Dental Insights $10K + FunBand $10K + Negocios & Etc. $10K + Sher Biomedical $10K + Carbo $5K + Jam $5K + CeleriBio $5K + Curaflow $5K + Bottle Rocket $1K + Maslow $1.5K = $82,500. Track placements verified against UCI News primary source 2026-05-12 (previously unknown per BACKLOG Priority 4).

disclosed_cash_totals_by_year_usd.2026_note: Confirmed cash for the 22nd annual edition (2026-05-21 finale): five tracks at $10,000/$5,000 ($75,000) + $20,000 Grand Prize (Pipeline) + $5,000 Runner-Up (NeuroStrat) + $2,500 MIE (Ascend First) + $1,000 Fan Favorite (RestWing) = $103,500. Excludes the $10,000 Beall Applied Innovation Specialty Award (LAMON), which Merage frames as a Wayfinder incubator and mentorship award of undisclosed cash-vs-in-kind form; counting it as cash would raise the total to $113,500. No standalone 2026 results press release exists; figures corroborated across the Merage NVC page and the OC Startup Council recap (2026-06-12).
disclosed_cash_totals_by_year_usd.2021_note: Updated 2026-05-13 to $61,000 per the NVC-2021-Report.pdf primary source ('$61,000 in CASH awarded to teams'). Earlier $41,000 figure underweighted track awards whose amounts were undisclosed in indexed press materials.
disclosed_cash_totals_by_year_usd.2020_note: Updated 2026-05-13 to $91,000 per the NVC-2019-2020-Report.pdf primary source ('Total cash awarded: $91,000'). Earlier $101,000 figure was inferred from press coverage that quoted Baby Box's combined $25,000 ($15K Grand Prize + $10K Consumer Products 1st) as if it were the standalone Grand Prize.
disclosed_cash_totals_by_year_usd.2018_note: Updated 2026-05-12: added Tech Surge 3rd ($5,000 LaserFocus) + 4th ($2,500 EchoSol) per Merage 2018 primary source. Prior total $92,500 missed these line items.
disclosed_cash_totals_by_year_usd.2017_note: Updated 2026-05-12 from $64,500: added Tech Surge honorable mentions Syntr Health Technologies ($2,500) + NanoShield Biotix ($2,500), Salux Diagnostics Outstanding Undergraduate Team award ($1,000), and the co-located Designing Solutions for Poverty Alleviation Blum Center runners-up Loy Loy ($5,000) + Pure Game ($5,000), and reclassifies Esqalate's $10K under that same co-located Blum Center competition.
disclosed_cash_totals_by_year_usd.2016_note: Added 2026-05-12: full track placements and prize amounts recovered from UCI News primary source. EmbryLux $20K dual-award + Dental Insights $10K + FunBand $10K + Negocios $10K + Sher Biomedical $10K + Carbo / Jam / CeleriBio / Curaflow $5K each + Bottle Rocket $1K + Maslow $1.5K.
disclosed_cash_totals_by_year_usd.2013_note: Added 2026-05-12: full sub-winner rosters and prize details recovered from Samueli engineering primary source. LAS $15K + ValveStrong $14K ($7.5K + $6K Orange Residency + $500 Concept Paper) + NoreGen $33.5K ($3.5K + $30K SoM Life Sciences Award) + Nobles $2.5K + Bloom Diagnostics $2.5K. Excludes $2,750 in-kind TechPortal Calit2 award to MEGAL!NE.
disclosed_cash_totals_by_year_usd.total_2017_to_2025_note: Updated 2026-05-12 to $821,000: previous $772,500 + 2017 +$21,000 (Tech Surge HMs, Outstanding Undergrad, Loy Loy/Pure Game Blum Center co-located) + 2018 +$7,500 (Tech Surge 3rd/4th). 2017 column now reflects the full slate listed in the Merage 2017 press release.
disclosed_cash_totals_by_year_usd.total_2017_to_2026_note: Added 2026-07-04: $821,000 (2017-2025) + $103,500 (2026 confirmed cash) = $924,500. Excludes the 2026 $10,000 Beall Applied Innovation specialty award pending confirmation it is cash rather than in-kind.
disclosed_cash_totals_by_year_usd.total_pre_2017_note: Pre-2017 cash totals are confirmed only for 2013 ($67,500) and 2016 ($82,500). 2009, 2012, 2014 partial totals are known but team-by-team rosters incomplete; 2010, 2011, 2015 remain structural gaps in the indexed public record.
